OPPOSING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFRL 2.7B AND PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF THE OPPOSITION NO LATER THAN MARCH 16, 2018 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, NOT REPORTED (D501)

ADDITIONALLY TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXISTS AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION AND AS TO THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. SEE MORAN DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MINI MINUTES FOR JUL-10-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF MADELINE MORAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR ORDER ENFORCING SETTLEMENT IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION TO ENFORCE THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT BY ORDERING THE DEFENDANT TO LIST THE PROPERTY FOR SALE AND/OR RE-FINANCE THE PROPERTY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS SHALL IMMEDIATELY (NO LATER THAN JULY 24, 2019) LIST THE PROPERTY FOR SALE. NO LATER THAN JULY 25, 2019 DEFENDANT SHALL FILE A DECLARATION OF COMPLIANCE DETAILING THE SPECIFICS OF THE LISTING. DEFENDANT SHALL ALSO PROCEED WITH THE REFINANCE EFFORTS. BY NO LATER THAN JULY 25, 2019 DEFENDANT SHALL FILE A DECLARATION DETAILING THE STATUS OF THE RE-FINANCING. IF THE RE-FINANCING IS COMPLETED BEFORE AN ACCEPTABLE OFFER IS MADE ON THE HOUSE, DEFENDANT MAY TAKE THE PROPERTY OFF THE MARKET. DEFENDANT SHALL FILE WEEKLY DECLARATIONS (ON WEDNESDAY OF EVERY WEEK STARTING ON JULY 31, 2019) DETAILING THE EFFORTS TO SELL THE PROPERTY (MLS LISTING/RE-LISTING; OPEN HOUSE, ETC.,) AND LISTING ALL OFFERS RECEIVED, INCLUDING COUNTER-OFFERS AND REASONS FOR NOT ACCEPTING THE OFFER, IF ANY. REQUEST FOR DAMAGES IS DENIED. SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT DOES NOT PROVIDE FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES ON CCP 664.6 MOTION. REASONABLY ATTORNEY'S FEES ARE GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,800. PLAINTIFF MAY APPLY FOR ADDITIONAL FEES AT THE CONCLUSION OF RE-FINANCE/SALE PROCESS UNLESS THE PARTIES AGREE ON THE AMOUNT OF FEES. DEFENDANT AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL ARE ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B IN FUTURE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2019 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the balance of the case dismissed pursuant to California Rule of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: GARRETT L. WONG; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Nov-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF MADELINE MORAN Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For Order Enforcing Settlement is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to enforce the settlement agreement by ordering the Defendant to list the property for sale and/or re-finance the property is GRANTED. Defendants shall immediately (no later than July 24, 2019) list the property for sale. No later than July 25, 2019 Defendant shall file a declaration of compliance detailing the specifics of the listing. Defendant shall also proceed with the refinance efforts. By no later than July 25, 2019 Defendant shall file a declaration detailing the status of the re-financing. If the re-financing is completed before an acceptable offer is made on the house, Defendant may take the property off the market. Defendant shall file weekly declarations (on Wednesday of every week starting on July 31, 2019) detailing the efforts to sell the property (MLS listing/re-listing; open house, etc.,) and listing all offers received, including counter-offers and reasons for not accepting the offer, if any.
Request for Damages is DENIED. Settlement agreement does not provide for recovery of damages on CCP 664.6 motion.
Reasonably attorney's fees are granted in the amount of $4,800. Plaintiff may apply for additional fees at the conclusion of re-finance/sale process unless the parties agree on the amount of fees.
Defendant and defendant's counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future.
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
